behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions (month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser validation/message logic inside one local component (MOCK-MISS: no cross-module handoff required). REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess in behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is explicitly about parser input validation: "parser rejects invalid expressions ... with a descriptive error message."
behaviors.test_description: Call parser directly with invalid cron strings and assert structured error type/message content for each invalid token/range case.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron expressions, including last day of month and nearest weekday.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets date-calculation correctness of one calculator boundary; failures are algorithmic edge cases (MOCK-MISS: no required module interaction beyond calculator). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on calculator output correctness, not parser/storage/executor handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate schedule objects (or parsed fixtures) and call next-run calculator from fixed start times; assert exact timestamp sequences for normal and edge expressions like LDOM and nearest-weekday.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags two schedules that fire within a configured minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at module boundaries: schedule expansion + gap comparison + conflict window reporting (MOCK-MISS: unit-testing only comparator can miss handoff/normalization defects). Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no AI/ML output dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM stage handoff. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text only describes local scheduler modules.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser/schedule objects with next-run generator and overlap detector in-process; feed two cron schedules plus min-gap config; assert detected conflict windows and non-conflict cases around boundary minutes.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when scheduler logic interacts with timezone/DST resolution and ambiguity policy, not just a single pure function (MOCK-MISS: stubbing timezone transitions can hide real rule-application bugs). Integration brake: (a) no required real subprocess/network; real tz handling is in-process library/data, (b) no AI/ML, (c) no multi-agentic sequence, (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejected System because behavior text does not require OS-level eventing or external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Run scheduler with real timezone rules in-process for known DST transition dates; assert expected handling for nonexistent 2:30 AM (spring) and duplicated 2:30 AM (fall) according to engine policy.

behaviors.description: If computed next run is already in the past, engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill).
behaviors.justification: Bug surface is orchestration/state handoff: next-run computation + current-time comparison + policy application (MOCK-MISS: isolated policy function test can miss integration with scheduler state). Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed, (b) no AI/ML dependency, (c) no multi-agentic steps, (d) no LLM-involved stage handoff. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow due absence of those dependencies in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In-process test with scheduler engine, fixed clock, and job configs for skip/backfill; simulate downtime by advancing clock past scheduled time and assert resulting queued/skipped executions.
